We present an efficient framework for low-capacity sequence models to encode signal-rich user-intent embeddings from episodic clickstream data. In production at Expedia, this work furnished a platform with downstream applications in content personalisation and contextual ranking. We demonstrate the idea using a lightweight BERT-inspired encoder architecture with task-specific prediction heads. Each head targets a specific e-commerce engagement outcome (e.g., browse, convert, bounce). At inference time, we decapitate the heads to expose the hidden representation in the shared backbone, from which we retrieve insightful engagement-aware embeddings. For low-capacity regimes, this approach outperformed equivalent GPT-style autoregressive models. Show less
